title: Your First Code 💻
description: Write your first line of JavaScript!

slides.title: Talking to the Computer 🗣️
slides.content:
To make the computer speak back to us, we use a special command:

`console.log()`

Whatever you put inside the parentheses `()` gets printed out for you to see.

quizData.question: Which command prints messages to the screen?
quizData.explanation: In JavaScript, `console.log()` is the standard way to output text for debugging or display.

slides.title: Try it out! ⌨️
slides.content:
See the code below? It says 'Hello World'.

Press **Run** to see the magic!

slides.title: Pro Tip 💡
slides.content:
## Text needs Quotes!

Notice the `'quotes'` around 'Hello World'?

Text (we call them **Strings**) must always be inside single `'` or double `"` quotes so the computer knows it's text, not code.

quizData.question: Which of these is correct text (String)?
quizData.explanation: Text must be wrapped in quotes to be treated as a String.
